summary: Iter6 backend + frontend tests. Backend: 6/6 new iter6 tests PASS (recap-ue formula, group_ids override, response shape, import-sessions enriched with semaine_souhaitee + nb_formateurs + group_ids + formateur_ids, schema persistence) + 10/10 iter5+iter4 regression PASS. Frontend: all 8 iter6 data-testids present in Planning Global (toggle-sidebar-aprog, day-all, day-toggle-0..4, a-programmer-sidebar-global). RecapHeures > Par UE tab renders formula header 'heures × nb_formateurs × nb_groupes', totaux globaux (75.0h / 87.0h temps formateur), 15 UE rows with pills par type, expansion révèle 'Temps formateur par type' + summary 'Voir le détail des séances/activités'. One CRITICAL frontend bug found and FIXED: PlanningGlobal line 607 crashed with 'RangeError: Invalid time value' when any day was deselected (used days[4] unconditionally — days is dynamic now). Patched to use allWeekDays[4] for the week-range label.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED IN-FLIGHT — format(days[4], ...) threw RangeError 'Invalid time value' whenever the user toggled off any of the 5 days because `days` (=selectedDayIdxs.map(...)) became shorter than 5. The fix swaps days[4] → allWeekDays[4] (header always shows Mon-Fri range). Without this, clicking day-toggle-0..4 immediately crashes the whole page with a runtime error overlay, blocking the entire iter6 day-selector feature.

data-testid='recap-ue-tab' is attached to the TabsContent panel (which is hidden when inactive) instead of the TabsTrigger. As a result, clicking the testid directly does not activate the tab (Playwright error: element not visible). The spec said 'onglet Par UE (data-testid recap-ue-tab)'. Recommend moving the data-testid to the <TabsTrigger value="ue"> element (or duplicating). Tab still works when clicked via text/role.

action_items: (Already fixed by testing agent) PlanningGlobal week-range label: use allWeekDays[4] instead of days[4] to avoid Invalid-time-value crash when day toggles are partial.
(Medium) Move data-testid='recap-ue-tab' from TabsContent to the corresponding TabsTrigger so the testid is clickable and visible (matches iter6 spec).

(Optional) The drop-restriction on bad promo/semestre is implemented via dropActivityOnDay guards (alert message) and isDropAllowed visual opacity-50 — could not E2E drag-test without grid-cell data-testids; only visual confirmation. Adding data-testid='day-cell-{promoId}-{dayStr}' would unlock E2E coverage of the new drop restriction.

(Optional) Coordination Fiches Projets table columns (Taille groupe / Groupes + Nb form. + act-nbform-* inputs) are only rendered inside each expanded UE group; consider initial expansion or expose count badges for easier test discovery.
critical_code_review_comments: PlanningGlobal.js now 898 LOC (was 836 — still > 700 LOC threshold). Should be split (e.g., extract DaySelector, SidebarAProgrammer, dropHandlers, headerToolbar).
server.py 2249 LOC — still well above 700 LOC seuil; routers/per-domain split still recommended.
Recap-ue endpoint duplicates the nb_groupes computation logic (sessions branch vs fiches branch). Could be extracted into a single helper that accepts an item dict.
import-sessions writes nb_formateurs = len(formateur_ids) or None — int(0) coerces to None which is acceptable, but the field type ends up mixed (int|None). Doc the convention or use 0.

Frontend day-toggle UI mutates `selectedDayIdxs` empty array but falls back to allWeekDays inside `days` derivation — good; but loadData (line 74) always reads allWeekDays[0..4] which is correct (loads full week regardless of UI filter).
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/pages/PlanningGlobal.js (one-line fix on header range label, days[0]/days[4] -> allWeekDays[0]/allWeekDays[4])

success_rate.frontend: 100% on observed iter6 toggles + RecapHeures Par UE (after fix)

seed_data_creation: All test fiches/sessions prefixed TEST_iter6_, cleaned up via try/finally (DELETE fiche/session and best-effort sweep of auto_imported fiches).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter6 backend validated end-to-end. Frontend Planning Global toggles + RecapHeures Par UE render correctly. One critical crash was fixed in PlanningGlobal.js (header used days[4] when days could be <5). Open items for main agent: (1) move data-testid='recap-ue-tab' from TabsContent to TabsTrigger; (2) optionally add day-cell data-testid to allow E2E drag-and-drop tests of the new drop-restriction (current alert-based negative path is logically correct but not playwright-testable without grid-cell selectors). Recap-ue formula validated: a=1h*1*1=1, b=1h*1*8=8, c=2h*2*4=16, group_ids override taille_groupe.
rca of the issue: PlanningGlobal.js bug RCA: in iter6 the developer introduced `selectedDayIdxs` state and a `days` array computed as map of selected indices, but the existing header label kept referencing days[4] (the original 5th element). When the user clicks any data-testid='day-toggle-N', days becomes length 4 and days[4] is undefined → date-fns format() throws 'RangeError: Invalid time value'. Reproduction: login → /planning-global → click any day-toggle button → red runtime error overlay appears. Mitigation: replaced `format(days[0]...)` and `format(days[4]...)` in the header span with allWeekDays equivalents so the label always shows the full Mon-Fri range regardless of toggle state. Verified: deselecting Lun no longer crashes; deselecting all 5 also OK (fallback to allWeekDays already protected `days`).
